From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

American actor (1922–2009)

| Harry Endo | |
| --- | --- |
| Harry Endo (1959) | |
| Born | (1922-07-20)July 20, 1922  [Colorado](/wiki/Colorado "Colorado"), U.S. |
| Died | January 9, 2009(2009-01-09) (aged 86)  [Brooklyn](/wiki/Brooklyn "Brooklyn"), [New York](/wiki/New_York_(state) "New York (state)"), U.S. |
| Occupation | Actor |
| Spouse | Myrtle Endo |
| Children | 2 |

**Harry Endo** (July 20, 1922 – January 9, 2009) was an American actor best known for his role playing Che Fong, a [forensic scientist](/wiki/Forensic_science "Forensic science") on the television series *[Hawaii Five-O](/wiki/Hawaii_Five-O_(1968_TV_series) "Hawaii Five-O (1968 TV series)")*.

Endo was born in [Colorado](/wiki/Colorado "Colorado"), but spent most of his life living in [Hawaii](/wiki/Hawaii "Hawaii"). He enlisted in the [United States Army](/wiki/United_States_Army "United States Army") in 1941 and served as a radio operator with an [infantry](/wiki/Infantry "Infantry") unit in Europe.[[1]](#cite_note-NewsdayObit-1)

He had been working for Territorial Savings Bank in Hawaii and was being filmed in a commercial for his employer when he was approached about an acting role playing "Che Fong" as one of the original cast members in the television series *[Hawaii Five-O](/wiki/Hawaii_Five-O_(1968_TV_series) "Hawaii Five-O (1968 TV series)")*.[[2]](#cite_note-AdvertiserObit-2) The series starred [Jack Lord](/wiki/Jack_Lord "Jack Lord") and [James MacArthur](/wiki/James_MacArthur "James MacArthur") as detectives for a fictional Hawaii state police department and ran from 1968 until 1980, making it the longest running crime show on American television until it was surpassed by *[Law & Order](/wiki/Law_%26_Order "Law & Order")* in 2003.[[1]](#cite_note-NewsdayObit-1)[[2]](#cite_note-AdvertiserObit-2) Actor Doug Mossman, who played the role of detective Frank Kamana on the show, described Endo's role in the program as portraying "the all-encompassing forensic genius, who was supposed to know everything about everything".[[2]](#cite_note-AdvertiserObit-2)

Fong appeared in 111 episodes of the crime drama from 1968 until 1977, and reprised his role in a 1997-hour-long made-for-television movie.[[3]](#cite_note-IMDb-3)

After the series ended, he played some minor roles, including two episodes of *[Magnum, P.I.](/wiki/Magnum,_P.I. "Magnum, P.I.")* and appeared in single episodes of both *[Jake and the Fatman](/wiki/Jake_and_the_Fatman "Jake and the Fatman")* and *[Murder, She Wrote](/wiki/Murder,_She_Wrote "Murder, She Wrote")*, before retiring from acting in the late 1980s.[[1]](#cite_note-NewsdayObit-1)[[3]](#cite_note-IMDb-3)

Endo died at age 86 on January 9, 2009, of a [stroke](/wiki/Stroke "Stroke") at the [Methodist Hospital](/wiki/New_York_Methodist_Hospital "New York Methodist Hospital") in [Brooklyn](/wiki/Brooklyn "Brooklyn"), [New York City](/wiki/New_York_City "New York City"). He was survived by his wife Myrtle, to whom he had been married for more than 60 years, and two children.[[1]](#cite_note-NewsdayObit-1) Myrtle Endo died in March, 2009 in [Brooklyn, New York](/wiki/Brooklyn,_New_York "Brooklyn, New York"). She was 85 years old.

## Partial filmography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Harry_Endo&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Partial filmography")]

* *[Hawaii Five-O](/wiki/Hawaii_Five-O_(1968_TV_series) "Hawaii Five-O (1968 TV series)")* (1969-1977) - Che Fong / Kaspar
* *Hawaii Five-0* (1997) - Che Fong (final film role)
